finding_text: Synaptic inhibition onto all excitatory neurons is reduced following sensory deprivation.
Cranial muscle activity prevented a direct investigation of neuronal gamma-band activity at the sensor level.
Alpha lateralization influenced behavioral performance positively, with both accuracy and speed of response improving as alpha lateralization increased.

Abnormally amplified beta oscillations in cortico-STN circuits do not result simply from an acute absence of dopamine receptor stimulation, but are instead delayed sequelae of chronic dopamine depletion.
Tonic-firing neurones were numerous in lamina I and deeper laminae but were not found in lamina II.
Nonneural signals are adequate to maintain circadian rhythms of clock gene expression in liver and kidney.

Individuals with a paucity of serotonergic and/or dopaminergic receptors and an increased rate of synaptic dopamine catabolism due to the high catabolic genotype of the COMT gene are predisposed to self-medicating behaviors.

During working memory failures, participants oriented their attention to the correct side of the screen (lateralized P1) and maintained covert attention to the correct side during the delay period (lateralized alpha power suppression).

During the VWM retention period, the amplitudes of theta/alpha-, high-alpha-, beta-, gamma-, and high-gamma-band oscillations were suppressed below baseline levels in occipital and occipitotemporal structures.
Blockade of beta-NA adrenoceptors did not block cocaine-induced reinstatement when given at either the BNST or CeA.
Phosphorylation within the AP2 binding motif in GABA(A)R beta subunits inhibits AP2 binding.

Many FEF corticotectal neurons carried signals thought to reflect cognitive operations, namely tonic discharges during the delay period of a delayed-saccade task (delay signals) or elevated discharges during the gap period of a gap task (gap increase signals).

Internet game overusers had increased glucose metabolism in the right middle orbitofrontal gyrus, left caudate nucleus, and right insula, and decreased metabolism in the bilateral postcentral gyrus, left precentral gyrus, and bilateral occipital regions compared to normal users.
wnt11-6/wntA/wnt4a undergoes feedback inhibition through canonical Wnt signaling but is likely to regulate brain size in a non-canonical pathway independently of beta-catenin-1 and APC.
Theta oscillations of septal GABAergic cells depend critically on a low-threshold, slowly inactivating potassium current.
Responses to thermal stimuli were found more commonly in WDR than in HT neurons: 75% of HT and 93% of WDR neurons responded to heat, while 16% of HT and 54% of WDR neurons responded to cold.

Treatment of rat hippocampal slices with the VGF-derived peptide TLQP62 resulted in transient potentiation through a mechanism that was selectively blocked by the BDNF scavenger TrkB-Fc, the Trk tyrosine kinase inhibitor K252a (100 nm), and tPA STOP.
In parietal, occipital, and temporal clusters, alpha suppression during the turning movement generalizes to more natural settings.
alpha-conotoxin MII blocks 34-49% of the nicotine-stimulated dopamine release in rat striatal synaptosomes.
The non-competitive NMDAR antagonist memantine blocked these radiation-induced alterations in cellular distribution.

The prominent rhythmic network activities of the hippocampus, specifically behavior-specific gamma and theta oscillations, are seen predominantly along the transverse and longitudinal axes respectively.
The decline of interneuron-mediated inhibition led to asynchronous downward transition across the cortical network and broke the regular slow oscillation pattern.
Depletion of ATX2 or expression of a mutant ATX2 protein that does not associate with polyadenylate-binding protein (PABP) suppressed behavioral rhythms and decreased PER abundance.
Spontaneous firing rates in areas V2 and V4 were 30-40% higher when attention was directed inside rather than outside the receptive field, even in the absence of stimuli.
A transient increase in excitability was observed after the washout of dopamine.
Almost 10% of the synapses impinging on GnRH neurons contained beta-endorphin.
Very fast oscillations became continuous when chemical synapses were blocked during K+ induced gamma (30–70 Hz) oscillations.
Early gamma oscillations (EGOs) synchronize neurons in a single thalamic barreloid and corresponding cortical barrel.
Weak corticothalamic feedback phase-locks spontaneous spindle oscillations in geniculate and perigeniculate nuclei.
Activation of 5-HT2A receptors is essential for tryptamine-based psychedelics to produce antidepressant-like effects in rodents.
Tonic EEG measures of cortical arousal were correlated with individual rates of error awareness.
Responses to large texture figures did not differ from responses to uniform-texture backgrounds for nearly all neurons in primary visual cortex (V1).
